Title: Overexpression of a Cotton Aquaporin Gene GhTIP1;1-like Confers Cold Tolerance in Transgenic Arabidopsis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GhTIP1;1-like is homologous to AtTIP1;1 and other TIP1;1 proteins across diverse plant species. Promoter deletion showed that the −33 to −1033 bp region containing an MYC cis-element is a core cold-responsive segment. Overexpression in Arabidopsis caused a complex senescence phenotype: early senescence symptoms in rosette leaves but delayed whole-plant senescence at the pod ripening stage. Although the functional assays for cold tolerance were performed in both cotton (VIGS) and Arabidopsis (overexpression), GhTIP1;1-like remains a cotton gene; Arabidopsis assays represent heterologous expression studies.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. GhTIP1;1-like encodes a tonoplast aquaporin of the TIP1;1 subfamily with conserved NPA motifs and six transmembrane helices and localizes to the vacuolar membrane.
2. GhTIP1;1-like is strongly induced by cold and also by heat, drought and salt stresses, with distinct tissue- and stage-specific expression patterns in cotton, and its promoter contains an MYC motif essential for cold-inducible activity.
3. Virus-induced silencing of GhTIP1;1-like in cotton seedlings reduces cold tolerance, increases O2− accumulation and MDA, decreases SS and SOD, and suppresses expression of cold-responsive genes GhCBF1/2/3 and GhKIN1.
4. Overexpression of GhTIP1;1-like in Arabidopsis accelerates bolting, increases seed number per pod, delays reproductive-stage senescence, and significantly enhances cold tolerance by improving physiological indices (higher SP and POD, lower MDA) and strongly up-regulating multiple cold-responsive genes (AtCBF1/2/3, AtCOR15a, AtKIN1/2, AtCOR47, AtRD29A).
5. Yeast two-hybrid assays show that GhTIP1;1-like physically interacts with GhBK1 and protochlorophyllide reductases GhPORA and GhPORC, and these interactors are differentially regulated by cold stress, implicating GhTIP1;1-like in a broader cold-response network.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Cold-stressed cotton seedling RNA-seq transcriptome (previous study) was used as a source to identify the cold-induced aquaporin gene GhTIP1;1-like; no new sequencing accessions are reported in this article.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GhTIP1;1-like acts as a positive regulator of plant cold tolerance: silencing in cotton reduces cold tolerance, whereas overexpression (tested in Arabidopsis) enhances tolerance to chilling/freezing stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Tonoplast aquaporin that likely modulates cellular water status and ROS homeostasis under cold stress: GhTIP1;1-like influences O2− accumulation, antioxidant enzyme activities (SOD, POD), soluble osmolytes (SS, SP) and lipid peroxidation (MDA) in cotton and Arabidopsis during chilling; its promoter contains an MYC cis-element that is critical for cold-inducible transcription; GhTIP1;1-like physically interacts with protochlorophyllide reductases (GhPORA, GhPORC) and GhBK1, which may integrate membrane transport, chloroplast function and BR signaling in cold responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Cold stress response pathway in cotton and Arabidopsis, involving activation of CBF and downstream COR/KIN/RD genes; GhTIP1;1-like protein also interacts with GhBK1, a protein associated with brassinosteroid (BR) signaling, suggesting a potential connection between aquaporin-mediated cold responses and BR-related pathways.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: In cotton under cold stress, silencing GhTIP1;1-like down-regulates expression of cold-responsive genes GhCBF1, GhCBF2, GhCBF3 and GhKIN1, indicating GhTIP1;1-like -->(activation under cold)--> GhCBF1/GhCBF2/GhCBF3/GhKIN1. In Arabidopsis under cold stress, overexpression of GhTIP1;1-like increases expression of AtCBF1, AtCBF2, AtCBF3, AtCOR15a, AtKIN2, AtKIN1, AtCOR47 and AtRD29A, corresponding to GhTIP1;1-like (heterologous expression in Arabidopsis) -->(activation under cold)--> AtCBF1/AtCBF2/AtCBF3/AtCOR15a/AtKIN1/AtKIN2/AtCOR47/AtRD29A.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tonoplast intrinsic protein (TIP) aquaporin of the major intrinsic protein (MIP) family; contains two NPA motifs and six hydrophobic α-helical transmembrane regions typical of tonoplast aquaporins
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuolar membrane (tonoplast); GhTIP1;1-like-GFP fusion localized to the vacuolar membrane / endomembrane system in onion and Nicotiana benthamiana epidermal cells
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhBK1 (BR signaling-associated protein), GhPORA (protochlorophyllide reductase A), and GhPORC (protochlorophyllide reductase C) physically interact with GhTIP1;1-like in yeast two-hybrid assays; other tested candidates (GhBZIP10, GhCAB7, GhTATC, GhCOLD, GhPSBY) did not show interaction. GhBK1 and GhPORA are cold-induced at the transcript level, whereas GhPORC expression is suppressed by cold.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In cotton, GhTIP1;1-like is differentially expressed among organs: highest in sepals, followed by 15 dpa fibers, petals, leaves, stems and roots, with low expression in 10 dpa fibers and stamens and the lowest in 5 dpa fibers. In 7-day-old seedlings (cotyledon stage), GhTIP1;1-like is induced by cold (4°C), heat (42°C), salt (300 mM NaCl) and dehydration (10% PEG6000), with expression peaking around 3 h in cotyledons and roots and stronger induction in cotyledons than roots. In 28-day-old seedlings (true leaf stage), basal expression is higher in roots than leaves; under cold, heat, salt and PEG treatments, expression in both roots and leaves increases then decreases, with stronger induction generally in roots; in leaves, heat and salt induce peaks at 3 h, whereas PEG-induced expression peaks at 24 h. ProGhTIP1;1-like::GUS transgenic Arabidopsis show promoter activity in leaves and roots at 22°C that is enhanced and extended to stems and anthers after cold (4°C), and the −33 to −1033 bp promoter fragment containing an MYC motif confers strong cold-responsive GUS expression, especially in roots, stems and anthers.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress at 4°C (for cotton seedlings, VIGS cotton, and Arabidopsis acclimation) and 0°C (prolonged cold treatment of Arabidopsis); heat stress at 42°C; salt stress with 300 mM NaCl; dehydration with 10% PEG6000; normal growth at 22–25°C; assays performed after 0–24 h (and up to 120 h for some cold treatments).
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Aquaporin function in plant abiotic stress responses, particularly cold tolerance; regulation of plant growth, bolting and senescence; effects on reproductive development and yield-related traits (pod length and seed number).
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold tolerance; responses to heat, drought and salt stress); developmental traits (bolting time, leaf senescence, reproductive growth and seed yield-related traits).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression of GhTIP1;1-like in Arabidopsis accelerates bolting, increases seed number per pod and (in line L2) pod length, and delays whole-plant senescence during the pod ripening stage despite early senescence symptoms in rosette leaves. In GhTIP1;1-like-silenced cotton, cold stress causes stronger leaf damage, higher O2− accumulation, lower SS content and SOD activity, and higher MDA levels. In transgenic Arabidopsis under cold stress, GhTIP1;1-like overexpression reduces O2− accumulation, increases soluble protein levels and POD activity, and lowers MDA content. Promoter analyses show that an MYC-containing fragment (−33 to −1033 bp) confers cold-inducible expression. GhTIP1;1-like expression is also strongly induced by heat, drought and salt stresses, indicating a broader role in abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Upland cotton (Gossypium hirsutum) cultivars TM-1, H559 and YM21 seedlings; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 wild type and GhTIP1;1-like-overexpressing lines (L2, L5) and ProGhTIP1;1-like::GUS reporter lines; Nicotiana benthamiana and onion epidermal cells for subcellular localization; Agrobacterium tumefaciens strains GV3101 and LBA4404; yeast strain Y2HGold for two-hybrid assays.
Plant_Genes.Variety: TM-1, H559 and YM21 upland cotton cultivars
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Cloning of GhTIP1;1-like ORF and promoter from cotton; protein sequence alignment and phylogenetic analysis; RT-qPCR to profile tissue-specific and stress-induced expression in cotton organs and seedlings; transient expression of 35S::GhTIP1;1-like-GFP in onion and Nicotiana benthamiana epidermal cells for subcellular localization; virus-induced gene silencing (TRV:GhTIP1;1-like) in cotton followed by cold treatment (4°C) to assess phenotypes, NBT staining for O2− accumulation, and measurements of soluble sugar (SS), superoxide dismutase (SOD) activity and malondialdehyde (MDA) content, plus RT-qPCR of cold-responsive genes (GhCBF1, GhCBF2, GhCBF3, GhKIN1); generation of Arabidopsis 35S::GhTIP1;1-like overexpression lines (L2, L5) and ProGhTIP1;1-like::GUS lines via floral dip plus PCR/RT-qPCR verification; phenotyping of Arabidopsis bolting time, pod length, seed number per pod and senescence; cold acclimation (4°C) and freezing (0°C) treatments of Arabidopsis followed by phenotype scoring, NBT staining, and determination of soluble protein (SP), POD activity and MDA content; RT-qPCR analysis of Arabidopsis cold-responsive genes (AtCBF1, AtCBF2, AtCBF3, AtCOR15a, AtKIN1, AtKIN2, AtCOR47, AtRD29A); yeast two-hybrid library screening using GhTIP1;1-like as bait, followed by pairwise Y2H validation of candidate interactors (GhBK1, GhPORA, GhPORC, etc.) and RT-qPCR of these genes under cold; promoter deletion analysis using ProGhTIP1;1-like F1–F5::GUS constructs and GUS histochemical staining under control (22°C) and cold (4°C) conditions; physiological index assays (POD, SOD, MDA, SS, SP).
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a cold-induced aquaporin gene homologous to AtTIP1;1 from a transcriptome database of cotton seedlings after cold stress; the full-length ORF was cloned from cold-treated TM-1 cotyledon cDNA, and a ~2.6 kb upstream promoter fragment was amplified from TM-1 genomic DNA for promoter analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GhTIP1;1-like is a promising target for improving cold tolerance in upland cotton: its silencing decreases, while its overexpression (tested in Arabidopsis) markedly enhances cold tolerance, accompanied by favorable ROS and antioxidant profiles and strong induction of cold-responsive genes. The gene also responds to heat, drought and salt, suggesting potential for broad-spectrum abiotic stress improvement. The MYC-containing cold-inducible promoter fragment could be exploited to drive stress-responsive expression of beneficial genes. Manipulating GhTIP1;1-like and its promoter via transgenic or genome-editing approaches could enhance seedling survival, maintain photosynthetic tissues under low temperature, and indirectly support yield stability in cotton grown in cold-prone regions.
